message:
fix(sync/history): drop the metadata-file handle before the post-write cleanup branch on Windows

`write_backup_metadata` opens the metadata sidecar with
`OpenOptions::new().write(true).create_new(true).open(...)` and
then runs an `(|| -> io::Result<()> { ... })()` closure that
serialises and writes the metadata.  On Windows, the file handle
held inside the closure's environment keeps the file mapped
exclusively even after the closure returns — and the
post-success cleanup branch immediately calls `fs::remove_file`,
which then fails with `ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION`.

Adding an explicit `drop(file)` between the closure and the
cleanup-on-error block releases the handle so `remove_file` can
unlink the metadata stub on the failure path.  Linux/macOS
allowed the unlink to succeed even with the handle held open
(unlink-on-close semantics), which is why this only repro'd on
the Windows CI runners.

Two-line patch with no behaviour change on Linux/macOS and a
correctness fix on Windows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
